title: Run Multiple Prompts in Stable Diffusion Quick Guide
description:
Hey everyone, welcome back to Pixaroma! Today we've got a quick guide on how to run multiple prompts in Stable Diffusion Automatic1111.

📋 What's Inside:
1️⃣ How to generate endless images with the same prompt
2️⃣ A neat trick to change one word in your prompt and get different images
3️⃣ How to use a script to run completely different prompts

👍 If this guide helps you out, make sure to hit the LIKE button and SUBSCRIBE for more cool tips and tricks in Stable Diffusion Automatic1111.

💬 Got any questions or future video ideas? Drop a comment below or ask in our Pixaroma Community Facebook group. https://www.facebook.com/groups/pixaromacrafts/

Thanks for tuning in, and have an awesome day! 🌟
transcript: Welcome back to the PixelRoma channel. In today's video, I'm going to show you some quick tricks  for running multiple prompts in stable diffusion automatic 11-11. Let's start with a simple prompt,  portrait of a cute penguin cartoon character, digital painting, adorable, north pole.  Hit the generate button and it'll make one image for you. But what if you want to keep making  new images with the same prompt without hitting generate each time? Just right click on the generate  button and choose generate forever. It'll keep making new images until you tell it to stop.  When you want to stop, right click on generate again and choose cancel generate forever.  Simple as that. Cool, let's say you want to make images with different animals but you don't  want to type a new prompt every time. Here's a neat trick using scripts. Scroll down to find a  script called XYZ plot and click it. In the X spot, you'll replace seed with prompt S R.  This means you're going to swap out words in your prompt.  Now, let's say your original prompt is about a penguin.  Type penguin first and then add other animals you want, like a cat, a dog, a lion, separated by commas.  Hit the generate button and boom.  You'll get a grid of images where the penguin is replaced by a cat, a dog, and a lion.  It's like a magic switcheroo and you can find all these new pictures in your output folder.  But what if you want to have a completely different prompt every time, not just a simple word  replacement? There's a script for that. Go back to the script and select prompts for file or text  box. You can enter your prompts here and press enter to receive a new prompt on each line.  Let's test it with portrait of a woman and portrait of a cat.  You can also upload a text file with all the prompts on separate lines.  For example, I've created a prompts.txt file where I've listed different prompts like a polar bear,  a penguin, and so on, each on a separate line. You can simply drag that file into the stable  diffusion interface. Once you're ready, click generate and stable diffusion will run all those  prompts for you. That's all for today. I hope you found this short tutorial helpful. If you did,  please consider liking and subscribing for more videos. Let me know what else you'd like to see  in future videos. You can leave a comment on YouTube or ask your questions in the PixelRoma  Community Facebook group. Thanks for watching and have a fantastic day.
